model_narrative: We plan the country's agriculture for one year across several farming regions. For each region we decide how many feddans to plant of each crop, how much temporary and family labor to use in each month, how much fodder to produce and move between regions, and how much of each nutrient to supply to livestock artificially. At the national level we decide the gross production of each commodity, how much to import and export, and where domestic consumption sits along each commodity's demand curve. The objective is to maximize the total consumer-and-producer surplus, which combines the welfare value of what people consume and the value of meat and milk, less the cost of crop inputs, hired and family labor, artificial nutrients, and moving straw between regions.

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the agricultural commodities (crops and processed products) the country can grow, trade and consume

sets.name: r
sets.doc: the five farming regions of the country, defined by their barrages; every region belongs to exactly one agro-climatic zone (upper, middle, or delta)

sets.name: tm
sets.doc: the twelve months of the agricultural year

sets.name: cn
sets.doc: the commodities that are sold on the national market and therefore have a domestic demand curve

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the fodder crops, whose by-products feed livestock

sets.name: ct
sets.doc: the fodder crops whose straw can be physically transported between regions

sets.name: nt
sets.doc: the two livestock nutrients tracked, protein and starch

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the three growing seasons; each vegetable crop belongs to one season

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grid points of the piecewise demand curve; each commodity's domestic demand is split into ten consecutive quantity segments

params.name: yld
params.doc: the marketable yield matrix in tons per 1000 feddans, giving how much of an output commodity comes from growing one unit of a crop in a region; the diagonal carries each crop's own yield and the off-diagonal rows carry oil and aggregate-commodity conversions. An (output, crop, region) triple is absent when that crop is not grown in that region

params.name: land
params.doc: the fraction of a feddan a crop occupies in a given zone and month; indexed by agro-climatic zone, not region. Absent when the crop is not in the field that month

params.name: laborm
params.doc: the labor a crop needs in man-days per feddan in a given zone and month; indexed by zone, not region

params.name: water
params.doc: the irrigation water a crop needs in a given zone and month, in the model's water units; indexed by zone, not region

params.name: feed
params.doc: the straw / by-product feed each fodder crop yields per unit grown in a zone, in 1000 tons per 1000 feddans; indexed by zone

params.name: cropdat
params.doc: per-crop technical coefficients; the protein and starch attributes give the nutrient content per ton of fodder as a percentage

params.name: agrreg
params.doc: regional figures; the area attribute is the cultivable land available in a region, and the protein and starch attributes are the region's livestock nutrient requirements. Absent attributes are zero

params.name: regwagm
params.doc: the average daily wage for a man in a region and month

params.name: netcs
params.doc: the per-unit non-labor input cost of growing a crop in a region (machinery, fertilizer, pesticide, seed, net of by-product credit)

params.name: qs
params.doc: the quantity at each demand-curve grid point: the domestic consumption level represented by segment g of a commodity's demand curve

params.name: ws
params.doc: the consumer-welfare value (area under the demand curve) accumulated up to grid point g of a commodity

params.name: pe
params.doc: the export price of a commodity

params.name: pm
params.doc: the import price of a commodity

params.name: los
params.doc: the post-harvest storage-loss fraction of a commodity; the share of gross production lost before it reaches the market. Absent means no loss

params.name: stran
params.doc: the cost of transferring one unit of straw from one region to another

params.name: veg
params.doc: the base vegetable-area figure for a region and season; the actual seasonal cap allows fifty percent above this base

params.name: grdf
params.doc: the minimum green-to-dry fodder ratio that regional fodder production must respect

params.name: day
params.doc: the number of working days in a month, used to convert a labor requirement expressed in man-days into man-months

params.name: prnut
params.doc: the per-unit cost of supplying a livestock nutrient artificially instead of from fodder

params.name: totmd
params.doc: the fixed value of meat and milk output, a constant added to the welfare objective

params.name: waterlim
params.doc: the total irrigation water available to the whole country over the year

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the area planted to each crop in each region, in 1000 feddans

vars.name: sales
vars.doc: the gross marketable production of each commodity, in 1000 tons

vars.name: imports
vars.doc: the quantity of each commodity imported, in 1000 tons

vars.name: exports
vars.doc: the quantity of each commodity exported, in 1000 tons

vars.name: natq
vars.doc: the weight placed on each demand-curve grid point of a commodity; these weights pick where on the piecewise demand curve domestic consumption sits and must form a convex combination

vars.name: fodder
vars.doc: the straw / by-product fodder available in a region from each crop, in 1000 tons

vars.name: trans
vars.doc: the straw of a transportable fodder crop moved from one region to another, in 1000 tons

vars.name: anut
vars.doc: the amount of each livestock nutrient supplied artificially in a region

vars.name: tlab
vars.doc: temporary hired labor used in a region and month, in 1000 man-months

vars.name: flab
vars.doc: family labor used in a region and month, in 1000 man-months

vars.name: cps
vars.doc: the total consumer-and-producer surplus, the welfare quantity the model maximizes

constraints.name: sales[cn]
constraints.intent: For each commodity sold on the national market, its gross marketable production cannot exceed the total marketable output obtained from the areas planted to the relevant crops across all regions.

constraints.name: los[cn])
constraints.intent: For each commodity sold on the national market, the supply available to the market must balance the demand for it. The gross production that survives storage loss, together with what is imported, must equal what is exported plus the total quantity consumed domestically along that commodity's demand curve.

constraints.name: natq[cn,
constraints.intent: For each commodity sold on the national market, the weights chosen across the points of its demand curve must add up to one, so that domestic consumption is a proper combination of the curve's quantity levels.

constraints.name: xcrop[r,
constraints.intent: In each region and each month, the total land taken up by the crops growing there must not exceed the cultivable area available in that region.

constraints.name: xcrop[r,
constraints.intent: In each region and each month, the labor required by the crops growing there, expressed in man-months, must be covered by the temporary labor and family labor used in that region that month.

constraints.name: xcrop[r,
constraints.intent: The total irrigation water used by all crops across every region and every month must not exceed the total water available to the country for the year.

constraints.name: veg[r,
constraints.intent: In each region and each growing season, the area planted to that season's vegetable crops must not exceed the maximum vegetable area allowed for the region in that season.

constraints.name: xcrop[r,
constraints.intent: In each region, the fodder available from each crop is the fodder its planted area yields in that region. For the fodder crops whose straw can be moved between regions, this is adjusted by adding the straw brought in from other regions and subtracting the straw sent out to other regions.

constraints.name: agrreg[r,
constraints.intent: In each region, the livestock requirement for each nutrient must be met. The nutrient supplied by the region's fodder, plus any of that nutrient provided artificially, must be at least the region's requirement for it.

constraints.name: fodder[r,
constraints.intent: In each region, green fodder cannot dominate the fodder mix. The region's green-fodder production, scaled down by the minimum green-to-dry ratio, must not exceed its dry-fodder production.

constraints.name: natq[cn,
constraints.intent: Define the total consumer-and-producer surplus. It equals the fixed value of meat and milk, plus the welfare value of domestic consumption and the value of exports net of imports across the marketed commodities, minus the cost of moving straw between regions, minus the cost of crop inputs over all regions and crops, minus the cost of artificially supplied nutrients, minus the cost of family and temporary labor over all regions and months.

query: We want to look at a national diversification target that holds total summer-maize area across all regions to 1,000 thousand feddans. What's the surplus the model reports with that limit in place?
expected_pyomo: model.smaize_total_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xcrop[r, 's-maize'] for r in model.r) <= 1000.0)

query: We need hard currency, and exports look thin in this plan. Couldn't we just commit to shipping at least 1,200 thousand tons abroad across all crops? What's the optimizer's reasoning for keeping exports as low as it does?
expected_pyomo: model.total_export_floor = Constraint(expr=sum(model.exports[c] for c in model.c) >= 1200.0)
